import React from 'react'; import { Sparkles, TrendingUp, Target, Award, MousePointerClick, ArrowRight, ScanLine, Code2, Globe2, Fuel, Zap, Star, Check, Search, Bot, BrainCircuit, Database, Share2, Layers, Infinity, Radio, Globe, Droplet } from 'lucide-react'; import { User } from '../types'; interface HomeProps { onNavigate: (view: 'analyze' | 'login' | 'recharge' | 'directory') => void; user: User | null; onDirectRecharge?: (packId: number) => void; } const Home: React.FC = ({ onNavigate, user, onDirectRecharge }) => { const handleStartAnalysis = () => { if (user) { onNavigate('analyze'); } else { onNavigate('login'); } }; const handleSelectPlan = (packId: number) => { if (user) { if (onDirectRecharge) { onDirectRecharge(packId); } else { onNavigate('recharge'); } } else { onNavigate('login'); } }; const plans = [ { id: 1, name: "Le Jerrican", credits: 50, price: 15, icon: , popular: false, features: ["Idéal pour tester", "1 Audit complet", "Valable 1 an"] }, { id: 2, name: "La Pompe", credits: 200, price: 49, icon: , popular: true, features: ["Pour optimiser sérieusement", "Support prioritaire", "+10% Bonus offert"] }, { id: 3, name: "La Citerne", credits: 1000, price: 199, icon: , popular: false, features: ["Usage intensif / Agence", "Accès API Beta", "+20% Bonus offert"] } ]; return (
{/* 1. HERO SECTION */}
{/* Background blobs - Purple */}
Nouvelle Génération de SEO
C

CitedBy.ai

La première plateforme d'optimisation AEO (Answer Engine Optimization). Analysez et influencez la façon dont ChatGPT, Claude et Gemini perçoivent votre marque.

{/* 2. FEATURES GRID (QUICK OVERVIEW) */}
{[ { icon: , title: "Simulateur de Perception", desc: "Visualisez comment les LLMs (GPT-4, Claude 3) 'voient' votre entité en temps réel." }, { icon: , title: "Code Sémantique", desc: "Générez automatiquement les données structurées (JSON-LD) que les robots préfèrent." }, { icon: , title: "Annuaire Indexé", desc: "Inscrivez votre marque dans le Knowledge Graph CitedBy pour forcer la découverte." } ].map((item, i) => (
{item.icon}

{item.title}

{item.desc}

))}
{/* 3. PEDAGOGICAL SECTION: SEO vs AEO COMPARISON */}

Le Changement de Paradigme

Nous passons de l'ère de la recherche (Google) à l'ère de la réponse (IA).

{/* VS Badge */}
VS
{/* SEO Classic Card */}

SEO Classique

  • Optimise pour des mots-clés.
  • Vise la liste de liens (SERP).
  • Trafic de "navigation" (Clicks).
  • Contenu long pour retenir l'utilisateur.
{/* AEO Modern Card */}
Le Futur

AEO & GEO

  • Optimise pour des entités et des faits.
  • Vise la réponse unique (Citations).
  • Trafic de "conviction" (Autorité).
  • Format "Answer-First" structuré pour les LLMs.
{/* 4. PEDAGOGICAL SECTION: THE 3 PILLARS */}

Les 3 Piliers de l'Autorité IA

Comment CitedBy structure votre présence pour convaincre les algorithmes.

{/* Pilier 1 */}

1. Identité (Graph)

Les IA ne raisonnent pas en pages web, mais en concepts. Nous définissons votre "Entité Nommée" dans le Knowledge Graph pour que GPT sache exactement qui vous êtes, ce que vous vendez, et votre légitimité.

{/* Pilier 2 */}

2. Sémantique (RAG)

Pour être cité, votre contenu doit être "ingérable". Nous transformons votre site avec le pattern Answer-First et des données structurées (JSON-LD) que les systèmes RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) adorent.

{/* Pilier 3 */}

3. Ubiquité (Signal)

Une IA confirme une information en la croisant. Nous multiplions les signaux de corroboration via notre Annuaire AEO et optimisons vos fichiers techniques (Robots.txt) pour faciliter le crawl IA.

{/* 5. PROCESS SECTION */}
{/* Background Lines */}

Votre parcours d'optimisation

{/* Step 1 */}
1

Analyse de Perception

Notre scanner interroge les principaux LLMs pour établir votre score de visibilité actuel et identifier les "trous" dans leur connaissance de votre marque.

{/* Step 2 */}
2

Génération du "Source of Truth"

Nous créons les fichiers llm.txt et llm.json qui serviront de référence canonique pour les robots d'indexation.

{/* Step 3 */}
3

Monitoring Continu

Les modèles d'IA changent chaque semaine. CitedBy surveille vos positions et vous alerte si une hallucination concernant votre marque apparaît.

{/* 6. PRICING SECTION - GTO GAS */}
Carburant Algorithmique

Rechargez votre GTO Gas

Notre monnaie virtuelle pour exécuter les audits, générer les correctifs de code et valider votre entité. Payez uniquement ce que vous consommez.

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
  - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
  - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
6. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
7. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
8. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
<analysis>
Let me chronologically analyze the entire conversation:

1. **Initial request**: User asked to "reprend le fichier" (take/review the file) at `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test`
   - I explored the project structure, read all key files, and provided a comprehensive summary
   - Project: CitedBy.ai - SaaS platform for AEO/GEO optimization
   - Stack: React 18 + TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS (CDN), Google GenAI, Supabase, Stripe

2. **"lance le dev server"**: User asked to start the dev server
   - Ran `npm run dev` in background
   - Vite started on port 3001 (3000 was busy)

3. **"ouvre-le dans le navigateur"**: User asked to open in browser
   - Used puppeteer to navigate to localhost:3001
   - Took screenshot showing the amber/yellow themed homepage

4. **"/senior-frontend"**: User invoked the senior-frontend skill
   - Skill loaded with component generator, bundle analyzer, frontend scaffolder capabilities

5. **Major request**: "améliore le frontend. rend plus professionnel en gardant toutes les fontionnalité. couleurs : noir/dégradé violet purple profond avec reflet metallique. ensuite analyse et optimise le bundle Vite. effectue une sauvegarde de la session actuelle pour pouvoir restaurer le site à son état actuel"
   - Three tasks created: backup, color theme redesign, bundle optimization

   **Backup**: 
   - Multiple PowerShell attempts with errors due to Windows path handling and the `nul` reserved file
   - Finally succeeded with `Copy-Item` + Remove-Item for node_modules
   - 266 files backed up to `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS-Backup-2026-02-12`

   **Color Theme Redesign**:
   - Updated `index.html` tailwind config: primary from `#fbbf24` to `#a855f7`, all amber colors to purple
   - Updated `tailwind.config.ts`: matching purple palette
   - Updated `App.tsx`: navbar, sidebar, footer, logo, buttons all to purple
   - Updated `ScoreRing.tsx`: color thresholds from emerald/amber to purple
   - Launched 7 parallel agents to update all 24 pages + 6 components + i18n
   - Additional cleanup agent for remaining `#1a1d21` references
   - All agents completed successfully

   **Bundle Optimization**:
   - Initial build: 1,325 kB single chunk
   - Added `manualChunks` in vite.config.ts: react, recharts, lucide-react, html2canvas, jspdf, genai
   - Added `React.lazy()` for 20 pages with `Suspense` wrapper
   - Final build: main chunk 85 kB (-93%), all chunks under 500 kB

6. **Latest request**: "execute pour avoir le meme frontend que le site suivant : https://ninjapromo.io/"
   - Analyzed ninjapromo.io via puppeteer screenshots and WebFetch
   - Key design elements identified:
     - Black pure background (#000000)
     - Floating pill navbar (rounded-full, bg-[#111111])
     - Gradient text (blue-purple-pink)
     - Pill-shaped CTA buttons with gradient
     - Dark cards (#111111) with subtle borders
     - Big bold typography for stats
     - Gradient border banners
     - Generous spacing
   
   - Created 3 tasks: core layout (#4), home page (#5), inner pages (#6)
   - Completed task #4: Updated index.html with NinjaPromo CSS classes (.gradient-text, .ninja-card, .gradient-border, .btn-gradient), App.tsx navbar (floating pill style), sidebar (darker #111111), footer, NavItem styling
   - Started task #5: Read Home.tsx fully, was about to rewrite it in NinjaPromo style
   - Task #6 still pending

Key files modified for NinjaPromo redesign so far:
- `index.html`: New CSS classes, background changed to pure black, new tailwind config colors (surface: #111111)
- `App.tsx`: Floating pill navbar, updated sidebar, footer, NavItem, mobile bar

Home.tsx was fully read but NOT yet modified for NinjaPromo style. This is where work was interrupted.

The CLAUDE.md rules state:
- NE PAS migrer vers Next.js
- NE PAS modifier services/analyzer.ts, services/criteria.ts, services/generator.ts
- NE PAS supprimer de fichiers existants
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User initially asked to review/load the project at `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test` (CitedBy.ai SaaS platform)
   - Start the Vite dev server and open it in the browser
   - Invoked `/senior-frontend` skill for frontend capabilities
   - **Major request 1**: Improve frontend professionally, change colors to black/deep purple gradient with metallic reflections, analyze and optimize Vite bundle, create a backup
   - **Major request 2 (most recent)**: Redesign the frontend to match the design of https://ninjapromo.io/ — a dark, modern SaaS site with floating pill navbar, gradient text, pill CTA buttons, dark cards, and bold typography

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - React 18 SPA with Vite (NOT Next.js — CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids migration)
   - Tailwind CSS via CDN (`cdn.tailwindcss.com`) with inline config in `index.html`
   - `tailwind.config.ts` for Vite build output
   - React.lazy() + Suspense for code splitting (20 lazy-loaded pages)
   - Vite `manualChunks` for vendor splitting (react, recharts, lucide-react, html2canvas, jspdf, genai)
   - NinjaPromo design patterns: floating pill navbar (`rounded-full`), gradient text CSS, `.ninja-card` (#111111 bg), `.gradient-border` (pseudo-element gradient), `.btn-gradient` (pill buttons), pure black background
   - CLAUDE.md rules: DO NOT modify `services/analyzer.ts`, `services/criteria.ts`, `services/generator.ts`; DO NOT delete files; DO NOT migrate to Next.js

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\index.html`**
     - Central theme file — Tailwind CDN config + global CSS styles
     - Updated twice: first for purple theme, then for NinjaPromo design system
     - Current state has NinjaPromo CSS classes:
     ```css
     .gradient-text { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #6366f1 0%, #a855f7 40%, #c084fc 70%, #e879f9 100%); -webkit-background-clip: text; ... }
     .ninja-card { background: #111111; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 1rem; }
     .gradient-border::before { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #6366f1, #a855f7, #e879f9); ... mask-composite: exclude; }
     .btn-gradient { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #7c3aed 0%, #a855f7 50%, #c084fc 100%); border-radius: 9999px; }
     ```
     - Tailwind config colors: background `#000000`, surface `#111111`, surfaceHighlight `#1a1a1a`, primary `#a855f7`

   - **`C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\App.tsx`**
     - Main app shell: navbar, sidebar, routing, footer
     - Updated for NinjaPromo floating pill navbar:
     ```jsx
     <nav className="sticky top-0 z-50 px-4 pt-3">
       <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto bg-[#111111] rounded-full px-6 h-14 flex items-center justify-between border border-white/[0.06] shadow-lg shadow-black/30">
     ```
     - Sidebar updated to `bg-[#111111]`, `border-white/[0.06]`, `w-64`
     - NavItem updated to cleaner style: `rounded-xl text-[14px] font-sans`, active state `bg-white/[0.08]`
     - Login button uses `btn-gradient` CSS class
     - 20 pages lazy-loaded with React.lazy() + Suspense with purple spinner
     - Footer: `bg-black border-white/[0.06]`
     - Mobile bar: `bg-black/90 border-white/[0.06]`

   - **`C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\vite.config.ts`**
     - Bundle optimization config added:
     ```ts
     build: {
       target: 'es2020',
       minify: 'esbuild',
       rollupOptions: {
         output: {
           manualChunks: {
             'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'],
             'vendor-charts': ['recharts'],
             'vendor-icons': ['lucide-react'],
             'vendor-html2canvas': ['html2canvas'],
             'vendor-jspdf': ['jspdf'],
             'vendor-genai': ['@google/genai'],
           }
         }
       },
       chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
     }
     ```

   - **`C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\tailwind.config.ts`**
     - Updated primary to `#a855f7`, secondary to `#7c3aed`, accent to `#c084fc`, background to `#050208`

   - **`C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\components\ScoreRing.tsx`**
     - Score colors changed from emerald/amber to purple shades

   - **`C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test\pages\Home.tsx`**
     - Was fully read (368 lines) as the current work item — needs NinjaPromo-style rewrite
     - Currently has purple theme but NOT yet NinjaPromo layout/typography

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **PowerShell backup errors**: `Copy-Item` failed with "container cannot be copied to leaf item" and `nul` reserved device name errors. Fixed by using `Copy-Item $src $dst -Recurse -Force` then removing node_modules/.next/dist after copy, accepting the `nul` file error as non-critical.
   - **Robocopy errors**: Path with spaces caused parameter parsing issues (`"E:/"` invalid parameter). Switched to PowerShell `Copy-Item` instead.
   - **Puppeteer selector error**: `button:has-text("Connexion")` is not valid CSS. Fixed by using `document.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(b => { if(b.textContent.includes('Connexion')) b.click() })`.
   - **Puppeteer variable redeclaration**: `const buttons` already declared. Fixed by wrapping in block scope `{ const btns2 = ... }`.
   - **Login form validation**: Form required email field to be filled before submission. Fixed by filling email and password fields via puppeteer before clicking submit.
   - **Edit tool "file not read" error**: Tried to edit GenerativeTools.tsx without reading first. Fixed by reading file before editing.
   - **Edit tool "replace_all" ambiguity**: Found 2 matches for `bg-[#1a1d21] border border-white/5` — fixed by setting `replace_all: true`.
